pages.raw_text:
Can the Washington Water
Power Co. be compelled to furnish
electric light on demand? Coun-
Gilman Pratt believes so, and to
that end last night introduced a
resolution requesting the corpora
ton counsel to look into the law
on the question and to report back
to the council at the next regular
meeting.


Corporation Counsel Hamblen
had little hesitancy in saying that
1t was a matter for determination
by civil suit, but Pratt was insis-
tent that the question should be
gone into officially and immediate
ly. He said many persons COM
planned that the company refused
to install lights when requested,
Notwithstanding there were poles
and wires in their vicinity and the
company would be put to little ex-
pense in furnishing the desired
service.


The company's reluctance to add
more lights is hard to understand
when II is remembered that sepa-
rate five years contract attaches to
each new light installed.
